title: Senior Technical Manager
description:
Job Overview:

The Senior Technical Manager is responsible for providing technical leadership and operational oversight for the Dry & Wet Process Division, which covers Toxic Industrial Waste (TIW) treatment, metal recovery, recycling (RCY), and all related operation processing activities. This role requires deep expertise in hazardous waste management, recycling technologies, process optimization, regulatory compliance, and strategic leadership. The incumbent will lead the operations team to drive productivity, efficiency, and sustainability in our operations while fostering continuous improvement and innovation. Additionally, Japanese language proficiency is required to facilitate effective communication with Headquarters in Japan and to ensure smooth reporting and collaboration.

Key Responsibilities,

1. Technical & Operational Leadership

  • Oversee the entire TIW-RCY process and ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements.
  • Lead the development, implementation, and optimization of waste treatment and recycling technologies.
  • Drive process improvements to enhance operational efficiency, reduce environmental impact, and optimize resource recovery.
  • Evaluate and transfer Japan technologies to local to improve waste treatment methods and recycling outcomes.

2. Operational Financial Management & Budgeting

  • Develop and manage the operations annual budget, ensuring alignment with the company’s financial objectives.
  • Monitor operating costs and expenses, leading operations team to implementing cost-saving initiatives without compromising operational efficiency.
  • Set and achieve budgeted EBIT targets, ensuring financial sustainability and profitability.
  • Identifying key areas for revenue growth and cost management.
  • Collaborate with the Finance & Administration department to track and report financial performance metrics.

3. Team Management & Leadership

  • Manage and mentor a team of Factory Managers, R&D Engineers and Technical Specialists, fostering a culture of continuous learning and professional development.
  • Participate in key operations meetings, including Daily Toolbox Meetings, Supervisors’ Meetings, Weekly Management & Sales Meetings, Project Meetings, and Safety Meetings and etc.
  • Provide technical guidance and problem-solving support to the team.
  • Promote teamwork and collaboration to achieve operational excellence.

4. Strategic Planning & Business Development

  • Collaborate with top management to develop and implement long-term strategies for waste recycling and sustainability initiatives.
  • Identify and develop new business opportunities, partnerships, and innovative recycling solutions.
  • Support the company’s sustainability goals by implementing circular economy principles in waste management.
  • Optimize costs and operational efficiency while maintaining environmental responsibility and compliance.

5. Safety & Risk Management

  • Collaborate with the EHS department to implement and oversee strict safety protocols, ensuring a safe working environment and compliance with regulatory standards.
  • Support risk assessments and hazard identification to ensure all equipment, processes, and personnel comply with safety, health, and environmental regulations.
  • Work closely with EHS to analyze incidents, lead root cause investigations, and implement corrective and preventive action plans to mitigate future risks.
  • Promote a safety is our first priority culture by reinforcing best practices and continuous improvement in hazardous waste handling and recycling operations


6. Japanese Reporting to Headquarters:

  • Ensure accurate and timely reporting to headquarters in Japan, including financial reports, operational updates, and strategic initiatives.
  • Coordinate with relevant departments to gather data and information for reporting purposes.
  • Facilitate communication and collaboration between the headquarters and local teams to support business objectives and alignment with global strategies.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, or related field). Master degree is a plus.
  • Previous experience in operation and project coordination, construction management, or related roles in the industrial sector.
  • Strong knowledge of Rotary Kiln and Vertical Stocker incineration technologies, WWTP, HF & CaF₂, and engineering principle
  • Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a multidisciplinary team and adapt to changing project requirements.

Benefits:

Competitive salary and benefits package.
Basic  : S$ 6,300 per month
Up to 4 Months Bonus.
Opportunities for professional development and career growth.
A dynamic and collaborative work environment.
Contributing to the success, growth, and technological advancement of a leading company in the industry.

postedCompany.description:
MODERN ASIA ENVIRONMENTAL HOLDINGS PTE LTD

DOWA launches the new Modern Asia Environmental Holdings

Merged company centralises waste management and recycling operations in Singapore hub

Singapore, 25 April 2019 – DOWA, the leading Japanese waste management and recycling solutions provider, announced today the formation of the new Modern Asia Environmental Holdings (MAEH) in Singapore to strengthen its innovative offerings locally and regionally. The merged entity spearheads DOWA’s strategic expansion across Southeast Asia by test-bedding cutting-edge solutions in Singapore and building a network of effective waste management and recycling capabilities across Southeast Asia.

The new MAEH brings together combined capabilities of Technochem Environmental Complex Pte Ltd (Technochem) and Dowa Eco-System Singapore Pte Ltd (ESG) under one umbrella to offer incineration, recycling and precious metal recovery capabilities as well as an end-to-end approach to help countries in the region address their growing environmental waste management and recycling needs. MAEH also has a 95-percent stake in PT Prasadha Pamunah Limbah Industri (PPLi), which provides waste collection, recycling, treatment and disposal services in Indonesia.

Technochem specialises in the collection of chemical and water treatment, industrial cleaning services, incineration of waste and the sale of solvents and chemicals. It runs DOWA’s first vertical combustion incinerator outside Japan and the first of its kind in Singapore and Southeast Asia at Tuas industry estate. This incinerator, which occupies a smaller physical footprint compared to other facilities, uses a breakthrough super-low air ratio combustion method that consumes less fossil fuels. It was designed by DOWA in Japan following the Great East Japanese earthquake in 2011 to clear massive amounts of debris at disaster sites. MAEH plans to build a similar facility in Indonesia through PPLi.

ESG located at Jurong industry park, on the other hand, carries out in precious metal recovery using hydrometallurgy process. The facility in Singapore can recover gold, silver and palladium from electronic waste, e.g. gold plating scraps, lead frames with plastic, printed circuit boards, ceramic substrates and press-cut waste. Together with DOWA’s facilities in Japan, the company can recover up to 22 elements from the electronic waste it receives.

President of MAEH, Mr. Hideaki Kobayashi, said, “Leveraging the pioneering technology and expertise of DOWA together with the streamlining of our operations in Singapore, we are in a strong position to better serve our customers here and in the region. We think we can make great contributions towards the creation of a zero-waste circular economy that will sustain the long-term development of Southeast Asia.”

For example, in Japan, DOWA has invented a creative way of managing ash, a by-product of incineration. Its proprietary ash smelting technology allows a simultaneous separation of heavy metals from ash in the recycling process, creating a molten sludge that can be used for road construction when combined with cement. MAEH has plans to introduce this technology to Singapore and the region.

“Singapore provides us with an excellent environment to test-bed some of our state-of-the-art waste management and recycling solutions. It is our hub for Southeast Asia and also our spring-boar
postedCompany.name: MODERN ASIA ENVIRONMENTAL HOLDINGS PTE. LTD.
